mission: Know Before You Go / Know Before You Respond
finalDetermination: Launch-focus portrait containment did not activate because applyLayoutMode() only applies the body gridly-launch-focus-portrait-validation class when the final mode is portrait and lastLayoutSignal.desktopGateSuppressedForLaunchFocus is true. Browser validation reached a portrait/mobile-visible state without that desktop-gate-suppression flag, so the body class was absent, the CSS selector never matched #gridlyPortraitV2, and the portrait root remained desktop-wide.
desktopGateOwner: CSS shows #gridlyDesktopGate from body[data-layout-mode=desktop]; resolveLayoutMode() suppresses that path by returning portrait only when forcedDesktop and shouldSuppressGridlyDesktopGateForLaunchFocus() are both true.
portraitRootOwner: #gridlyPortraitV2 is static markup; body[data-layout-mode=portrait] CSS displays it; applyLayoutMode() calls activateGridlyPortraitV2StartupOwner() in portrait mode.
containmentOwner: applyLayoutMode() toggles gridly-launch-focus-portrait-validation on body; CSS expects body[data-layout-mode=portrait].gridly-launch-focus-portrait-validation #gridlyPortraitV2.
whyContainmentClassPresentFalse: The class is gated by lastLayoutSignal.desktopGateSuppressedForLaunchFocus rather than by final launch-focus portrait validation. That flag is only true on the forcedDesktop suppression branch; it is false when portrait is chosen or retained for width/container/overflow reasons.
whyPortraitMaxAllowedWidthWas980: The V747 audit computes portraitMaxAllowedWidth as 430 only when isGridlyLaunchFocusPortraitValidationActive() is true. Because the body containment class was absent and lastLayoutSignal.desktopGateSuppressedForLaunchFocus was false, launchFocusPortraitValidation was false and the fallback Math.min(viewportWidth || portraitRootWidth || 980, 980) produced 980.
operationsAssessment: Operations Center remains disabled and its shell/data bridge do not own body layout mode, desktop-gate CSS, #gridlyPortraitV2 visibility, or the containment class. The issue is an older desktop-gate/layout-mode coupling exposed by suppressing the desktop gate.
recommendedNextFix: In a later behavior-change milestone, make applyLayoutMode() apply the body containment class whenever launch-focus mobile validation is active and the resolved final layout is portrait, instead of requiring desktopGateSuppressedForLaunchFocus to be true. V748 intentionally does not patch behavior.
